bio: Aaron Kipkirui Cheruiyot is a Kenyan politician and the Senator for Kericho County under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). He succeeded Charles Keter, who had resigned to take up a cabinet portfolio, and has represented Kericho in the Senate since 2022, in the 13th Parliament. In 2022 his fellow senators elected him Majority Leader of the Senate, a position that places him at the center of steering government business through the upper house. Bearing the honorific MGH (Moran of the Order of the Grand Warrior), Cheruiyot has built his political profile around Kericho's tea-growing communities and his leadership role in the chamber.

Abass Sheikh Mohamed is a Kenyan politician who has represented Wajir in Parliament across two chambers. He first entered the National Assembly in 2013 as MP for Wajir East on a United Republican Party ticket, backed by the Jubilee Coalition, serving through 2017. In 2022 he moved to the Senate, winning the Wajir County seat on a United Democratic Movement (UDM) ticket for a term running from August 2022 to July 2027. His career reflects a shift from constituency to county-wide representation within Kenya's devolved system, spanning the 11th and 13th Parliaments.

Abdi Omar Shurie is a Kenyan politician representing Balambala Constituency in Garissa County under the Jubilee Party. First elected to the National Assembly in 2017 and re-elected in 2022, he has served continuously across the 12th and 13th Parliaments. In the 13th Parliament he chairs the Public Debt and Privatisation Committee, also sitting on the Appointments and Liaison committees, giving him a hand in overseeing the country's debt management and privatisation policy. In 2024 President William Ruto honoured him with the 1st Class Chief of the Order of the Burning Spear (CBS), one of Kenya's top state commendations.

Abdi Omar Shurie is a Kenyan politician from the Jubilee Party who has represented Balambala Constituency in Garissa County in Kenya's National Assembly. He was first elected in 2017, serving in the 12th Parliament, and was re-elected in 2022. In 2024 President William Ruto conferred on him the 1st Class Chief of the Order of the Burning Spear (CBS) award, recognising his service in Parliament.

Abdikadir Hussein is a Kenyan politician who has represented Lagdera Constituency in the National Assembly during the 13th Parliament under the Orange Democratic Movement. Earlier in his career, running on the Safina party ticket, he was elected to represent Mandera Central Constituency in the 2007 general election, defeating the sitting MP Billow Kerrow. In the 13th Parliament, he serves on the Blue Economy, Water and Irrigation Committee and the Defence, Intelligence and Foreign Relations Committee.

Hussein Maalim Mohamed is a Kenyan politician who represented Dujis Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament under KANU. He also served as a Minister of State in the Office of the President.

Abdikadir Omar Aden is a Kenyan politician who represented Balambala Constituency in Garissa County in the 11th Parliament, elected in 2013 on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. During his single term he served on the Public Accounts Committee (PAC), scrutinising public spending. He lost his bid for re-election in 2017, ending his time in the National Assembly after one term.

Abdikadir Ore Ahmed is a Kenyan health professional and politician who represented Wajir West Constituency in the 11th Parliament. He was elected in 2013 on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket with the backing of the CORD Coalition, serving a single term in the National Assembly.

Abdul Rahim Dawood is a Kenyan businessman and politician who has represented North Imenti Constituency in Meru County across three Parliaments. First elected in 2013 on an Alliance Party of Kenya ticket, he was re-elected in 2017 under the Jubilee Party and again in 2022, this time as an Independent, for a term running to 2027. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Departmental Committee on Transport and Infrastructure and the Special Funds Accounts Committee, having previously chaired a committee in the 12th Parliament and served on Departmental Committees and the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee in the 11th.

Abdulaziz Ali Farah is a Kenyan politician who represented Mandera East Constituency in the 11th Parliament, elected in 2013 on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket with the backing of the Jubilee Coalition. He served on the Environment and Natural Resources Committee. A member of the Murulle clan, he relied on the endorsement of his clan's council of elders to win his first term but failed to secure their backing for a second, ending his parliamentary career in 2017. He graduated from Kenyatta University in 1995.

Abdullahi Jaldesa Banticha is a Kenyan politician who represented Isiolo South Constituency in the 11th Parliament, elected in 2013 on a United Republican Party ticket with the support of the Jubilee Coalition, succeeding KANU's Abdul Ali. He served on the House Committee on Energy, Communication and Information. A 2016 Mzalendo Trust report noted he spoke only twice on the floor during the term.

Abdulswamad Sheriff Nassir is a Kenyan politician and the Governor of Mombasa County, elected in 2022 on an ODM ticket under the Azimio la Umoja-One Kenya Alliance. Before becoming governor he represented Mvita Constituency in the National Assembly from 2013 to 2022, spanning the 11th and 12th Parliaments, where he sat on Departmental Committees and the Public Investments Committee. He currently serves as deputy party leader of ODM.

Adan Keynan Wehliye is a Kenyan politician and the longest-serving member of the National Assembly, known as the 'Father of the House.' First elected in 1997 to represent Wajir West, he served that constituency again from 2007 to 2013 before it was split to create Eldas, which he has represented since, across the 11th, 12th and 13th Parliaments. He sat with the Orange Democratic Movement until 2017, when he switched to the Jubilee Party, under which he was re-elected in 2022 for a term running to 2027. In the 13th Parliament he serves on the Budget and Appropriations, House Business, Public Investments and Selection Committees, having earlier sat on Departmental Committees and Powers and Privileges in the 11th.

Adan Mohamed Nooru is a Kenyan politician who has represented Mandera North across decades of Kenyan politics. He was first elected to Parliament in 1983 during the one-party era under the Kenya African National Union (KANU), later serving as an assistant minister in the KANU government. He returned to Parliament in 2013 as MP for Mandera North on a United Republican Party ticket, sitting in the 11th Parliament on the Budget and Appropriations and Departmental Committees. He has also served as a member of the East African Legislative Assembly.

Peter Adhu Awiti was a Kenyan politician who represented Karachuonyo Constituency in the National Assembly from 1997 until 2007, serving under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) banner during the 9th Parliament.

Agnes Kavindu Muthama is a Kenyan politician who has served as Senator for Machakos County on a Wiper ticket since being elected on 19 March 2021, continuing in the role through the 13th Parliament. Before entering the Senate she served on the Tanathi Water Services Board from 2018 to 2021, giving her a background in water sector governance ahead of her national political career.

Agnes Mantaine Pareyio is a Kenyan politician and longtime Maasai women's rights and anti-female genital cutting activist. She founded and directs the Tasaru Ntomonok Rescue Center for Girls, an organisation campaigning against female genital mutilation, and was named UN Person of the Year in 2005 for this work. In 2022 she became the first woman elected MP for Narok North Constituency, representing the Jubilee Party in the 13th Parliament, where she serves on the National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity and Social Protection Committees.

professional.title: Founder and Director
professional.institution: Tasaru Ntomonok Rescue Center for Girls

bio: Ahmed Abdisalan Ibrahim is a Kenyan politician who served in the 11th Parliament as MP for Garsen Constituency on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket, elected in 2013, and later represented Wajir North Constituency for the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) in the 12th Parliament. He is known as an activist against harmful cultural practices, including early child marriage, campaigning against the practice within his community.

Ahmed Ibrahim Abass is a Kenyan politician who represented Ijara Constituency in Garissa County in Kenya's National Assembly. He was a member of the 11th Parliament, having been elected to the seat on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket.
Ibrahim Ahmed Abass is a Kenyan politician who represented Ijara Constituency, in Garissa County, in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament under the Orange Democratic Movement.

Aisha Jumwa Karisa Katana is a Kenyan politician who rose from local government to national office. She served as Councillor for Takaungu Ward from 1997 to 2007, then as Woman Representative for Kilifi County from 2013 to 2017 on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. She went on to represent Malindi Constituency in the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament while also serving as a Commissioner of the Parliamentary Service Commission from 2017 to 2022. In 2022 she was appointed Cabinet Secretary for Gender, a post from which President William Ruto dismissed her on 11 July 2024. She holds a Bachelor's degree in Leadership and Management from the International Leadership University and an Executive Diploma in Governance from Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology.

education.title: Bachelor's degree in Leadership and Management
Executive Diploma in Governance
education.institution: Jomo Kenyatta University of Agriculture and Technology

bio: Mohamed Ali Mohamed, popularly known by his nickname Moha Jicho Pevu, is a Kenyan politician, media personality, and television anchor. He has represented Nyali Constituency in the National Assembly across the 12th and 13th Parliaments, running as an independent before joining the United Democratic Alliance, and earlier represented Mandera West Constituency in the 11th Parliament under the United Republican Party. In the 13th Parliament, he serves on the Committee on Implementation and the Committee on Environment, Forestry and Mining.

Mohamed Maalim Mohamud is a Kenyan politician who represented Mandera West Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament under URP, serving from 2007 to 2013. He later served as a Senator for Mandera County, where he chaired the Senate's Finance and Budget Committee, and he is affiliated with the Jubilee Party. Earlier in his career, from 2008 to 2011, he served as Assistant Minister in the Ministry of Energy.

Ali Wario is a Kenyan politician who has represented Tana River County constituencies across four Parliaments. He first won the Bura Constituency seat in 2002 on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket in the 9th Parliament, serving as Assistant Minister for Home Affairs from 2003 to 2006 before being redeployed as Assistant Minister for Special Programmes. After losing his seat in 2007, he reclaimed it in 2013 on a The National Alliance ticket within the Jubilee Coalition for the 11th Parliament, sitting on the Departmental Committee on Public Works, Roads and Transport. He was elected MP for Garsen Constituency on an ODM ticket in the 13th Parliament, where he serves on a Departmental Committee.

Ali Wario is a Kenyan politician who represented Bura Constituency in Tana River County during the 11th Parliament on The National Alliance ticket, within the Jubilee Coalition. He went on to serve in the National Assembly's 13th Parliament representing Garsen Constituency on an Orange Democratic Movement (Azimio) ticket.

Ali Wario is a Kenyan politician who has served in Kenya's 9th and 11th Parliaments. He was elected in 2002 on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket, serving as Assistant Minister for Home Affairs from 2003 to 2006 and then Assistant Minister for Special Programmes. After losing re-election in 2007, he returned to Parliament in 2013 on The National Alliance (TNA) ticket within the Jubilee Coalition, sitting on the Departmental Committee on Public Works, Roads and Transport.

Ali Wario is a Kenyan politician who won the Bura Constituency seat in Tana River County in the 2002 general election on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket, serving in the 9th Parliament. During this period he was appointed Assistant Minister for Home Affairs, holding the post from 2003 to 2006 before being redeployed as Assistant Minister for Special Programmes. He lost his re-election bid in 2007 but later returned to Parliament in 2013.

Alice Muthoni Wahome is a Kenyan politician who represented Kandara Constituency in Murang'a County across three Parliaments, first elected in the March 2013 general election and successfully defending her seat in 2017 and 2022. Initially elected on a The National Alliance ticket, she later moved through the Jubilee Party to the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). In 2022 she resigned her parliamentary seat to become Cabinet Secretary for Lands, Public Works, Housing and Urban Development, a position from which she was dismissed in July 2024.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Lands, Public Works, Housing and Urban Development

bio: Alice Wambui Ng'ang'a is a Kenyan politician who represents Thika Town Constituency in Kiambu County. She was first elected in the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket and returned to the National Assembly in 2022 on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament, where she chairs the Departmental Committee on Social Protection and also sits on the Liaison Committee, overseeing both social welfare policy and the scheduling of parliamentary business across her two non-consecutive terms.

Amina Laura Mnyazi is a Kenyan politician who represents Malindi Constituency in Kilifi County, elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket in August 2022. A first-term member of the 13th Parliament, she serves on the Departmental Committee on Blue Economy, Water and Irrigation as well as the Delegated Legislation Committee, reflecting an interest in the coastal county's marine and water resource policy.

Amos Mūhinga Kīmunya is a Kenyan politician who represented Kipipiri Constituency across the 9th and 12th Parliaments, first elected under NARC and later under the Jubilee Party. He served as Minister of Lands and Settlement before becoming Minister of Finance in 2006, resigning in July 2008 amid the Grand Regency Hotel scandal, and later served as Minister of Trade. In the 12th Parliament he chaired a Departmental Committee and sat on the Constitution and House Business Committees before becoming Majority Leader of the National Assembly under the Jubilee Party, replacing Aden Duale in that role. In 2019 he earned a PhD in Business Administration from the United States International University in Nairobi.

Anab Mohamed Gure is a Kenyan politician who served as Women's Representative for Garissa County in the National Assembly from 31 August 2017 to 2022, sitting in the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party. Before entering elective politics she worked as a Research Assistant at the Kenya National Bureau of Statistics and as a Civic Education Officer with the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC), roles that gave her grounding in public data and electoral processes ahead of her time representing Garissa women in Parliament.

Andrew Mwadime is a Kenyan politician who represented Mwatate Constituency in Taita Taveta County across the 11th and 12th Parliaments on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. He left the National Assembly to run for Governor of Taita Taveta County, resigning from the Communist Party of Kenya to contest as an Independent candidate and winning the governorship in August 2022.

Annie Wanjiku Kibeh is a Kenyan politician and former media practitioner who represented Gatundu North Constituency in Kiambu County in the 12th Parliament, elected in the 2017 general election on a Jubilee Party ticket. She served a single term in the National Assembly from 2017 to 2022.

Antoney Kibagendi is a Kenyan politician who represents Kitutu Chache South Constituency in Kisii County. Elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket, he sits in the 13th Parliament, where he serves on the Health Committee.

Anthony Njoroge Wainaina is a Kenyan politician who represents Kieni Constituency in Nyeri County. Elected in 2022 on a United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket, he sits in the 13th Parliament, where he serves on the Communication, Information and Innovation Committee, the National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity Committee, and the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee.

Arthur Kinyanjui Magugu was a Kenyan politician who represented Githunguri Constituency in two separate stretches, from 1969 to 1992 and again from 2002 to 2007, the latter recorded in the 9th Parliament under KANU. He served as Kenya's Minister for Finance from 1982 to 1988. Earlier in life he was a beneficiary of the 1959 Kennedy Airlift, which sent young East Africans to study in the United States.

Asman Abongutum Kamama is a Kenyan politician who represented Baringo East Constituency in the National Assembly from the 2007 general election, running with the Party of National Unity through its NARC-Kenya coalition partner in the 9th Parliament, and later represented Tiaty Constituency for the United Republican Party in the 11th Parliament. He lost his bid for re-election in 2017, garnering 34 percent of the vote.

Badi Twalib is a Kenyan politician who represented Jomvu Constituency in Mombasa County in the 11th Parliament, first elected in 2013. He served on the Regional Integration Committee and the Joint Parliamentary Broadcasting and Library Committee during his term.

Bady Bady Twalib is a Kenyan politician who represents Jomvu Constituency in Mombasa County. First elected in 2013 on a Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket, he moved to the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) and has been re-elected in 2017 and 2022, now serving his third consecutive term in the 13th Parliament. He won the 2022 general election with 13,850 votes, defeating Jubilee Party's Renson Thoya, and sits on a Departmental Committee and the Transport and Infrastructure Committee.

Adagala Beatrice Kahai is a Kenyan politician who serves as Woman Representative for Vihiga County in the National Assembly. First elected in 2017 on an Amani National Congress (ANC) ticket and re-elected in 2022 with 52,507 votes, she is now in her second term in the 13th Parliament, where she sits on the Public Investments Committee, the Trade, Industry and Cooperatives Committee, and the Selection Committee. She earlier represented Tharaka Nithi County as Woman Representative in the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket.

Beatrice Kadeveresia Elachi is a Kenyan politician who represents Dagoretti North Constituency in Nairobi County, elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket to the 13th Parliament, where she serves on a Departmental Committee, the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee, and the Public Petitions Committee. Before her election to the National Assembly, she served as Speaker of the Nairobi County Assembly from 6 September 2017 until her resignation on 11 August 2020, succeeding Alex Ole Magelo, and immediately before that she was Majority Whip in the Kenya Senate.

Beatrice Nkatha Nyaga was a Kenyan politician who served as Woman Representative for Tharaka Nithi County in the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party, having earlier represented Tharaka in the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket.
Beatrice Pauline Cherono Kones was a Kenyan politician who represented Bomet East Constituency in the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party.

Benard Kipsengeret Koros is a Kenyan politician who represented Sigowet-Soin Constituency in Kericho County during the 12th Parliament. He was elected in 2017 as an Independent candidate, garnering 20,284 votes against the outgoing member's 13,068, defeating Justice Kemei to become the second Member of Parliament for the constituency since its creation in 2013.
Benard Otieno Okoth is a Kenyan politician who represented Kibra Constituency in Nairobi County during the 12th Parliament on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket.

Benjamin Kipkirui Langat is a Kenyan politician and diplomat who represents Ainamoi Constituency in Kericho County. He first served as MP for Ainamoi from 2008 to 2017, spanning the 10th and 11th Parliaments, sitting on the Mediation Committee, before returning to the seat in the 13th Parliament under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), where he serves on the Finance and National Planning, Public Investments and Departmental Committees. Between his parliamentary terms he served as Kenya's High Commissioner to Namibia. He holds a Bachelor of Commerce degree and a Master of Business Administration in Accounting, both from the University of Nairobi, and is a Certified Public Accountant and member of the Institute of Certified Public Accountants of Kenya.

education.title: Master of Business Administration in Accounting

professional.title: Certified Public Accountant
professional.institution: Institute of Certified Public Accountants of Kenya

bio: Benjamin Mwangi is a Kenyan politician who represents Embakasi Central Constituency in Nairobi County. First elected in 2017 under the Jubilee Party for the 12th Parliament, he was re-elected in 2022 under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) for the 13th Parliament, now serving his second term, where he sits on the Constitutional Implementation Oversight Committee and a Departmental Committee.

Benjamin Jomo Washiali is a Kenyan politician who represented Mumias East Constituency in Kakamega County across several political affiliations. He was first elected in the 2007 general election, later represented the seat under the United Democratic Forum in the 11th Parliament, and was re-elected in 2017 under the Jubilee Party for the 12th Parliament, where he served on a Departmental Committee.

Benson Itwiku Mbai is a Kenyan politician who has represented Masinga Constituency in Machakos County since the 2007 general election, serving in the 9th Parliament under NARC and later the 11th Parliament under the Forum for Restoration of Democracy-People, sitting on a Departmental Committee. He has also been associated with the Orange Democratic Movement-Kenya.

Bernard Kitungi is a Kenyan politician who represented Mwingi West Constituency in Kitui County during the 11th Parliament. He was elected in 2013 on a Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya (WDM-K) ticket with the support of the CORD Coalition.
Beth Wambūi Mūgo is a Kenyan politician who represented Dagoretti Constituency in Nairobi in the 9th Parliament, elected in the 2007 Kenyan parliamentary election on a Party of National Unity ticket.

Bonaya Adhi Godana was a Kenyan politician who represented North Horr Constituency on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket in the 9th Parliament. He served as Kenya's Minister for Foreign Affairs from January 1998 until 2001.
Bonny Khalwale is a Kenyan politician and medical doctor who serves as Senator for Kakamega County under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), representing the county in the 13th Parliament.
Bonny Khalwale is a Kenyan politician and medical doctor who represented Ikolomani Constituency in the 9th Parliament on a NARC ticket, before later becoming Senator for Kakamega County.

Ali Roba, born Ibrahim Ali Roba, was the first governor of Mandera County, winning election in March 2013 on a URP ticket within the Jubilee coalition and securing a second and final term in August 2017. After completing two terms as governor, he moved to represent Mandera County in the Senate, where his 13th Parliament term is affiliated with the UDM.

Wambilianga Catherine Nanjala is a Kenyan politician who serves as Woman Representative for Bungoma County under the Forum for Restoration of Democracy-Kenya (FORD-Kenya) party. First elected in 2017 and re-elected in 2022, she is serving her second term, running to 2027, in the 13th Parliament, where she sits on the Labour Committee and the National Government Constituencies Development Fund Committee.

Catherine Wanjiku Waruguru is a Kenyan politician from Laikipia County who was elected Woman Representative for Laikipia County in the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party. She earlier served as a nominated member of the first Laikipia County Assembly.

Charity Kaluki Ngilu is a Kenyan politician who represented Kitui Central Constituency in the 9th Parliament under NARC. She unsuccessfully ran for President of Kenya in 1997. She went on to serve as Minister for Health from 2003 until 2007 and Minister for Water and Irrigation from April 2008 to 2013, and later as Cabinet Secretary for Land, Housing and Urban Development from 2013 until 2015. She subsequently became the second elected Governor of Kitui County.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Land, Housing and Urban Development

bio: Charles Cheruiyot Keter is a Kenyan politician who represented Belgut Constituency in Kericho County during the 9th Parliament on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket. He has been a member of the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) since 2022.

Muriuki Charles Njagagua is a Kenyan lawyer and politician who represented Mbeere North Constituency in Embu County. He was first elected in the 11th Parliament on an Alliance Party of Kenya ticket and was re-elected under the Jubilee Party for the 12th Parliament.

Charles Mutisya Nyamai is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kitui County in the National Assembly, associated with the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC) since the 2007 general election, and later representing Kitui Rural Constituency on a Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya (WDM-K) ticket in the 11th Parliament.

Charles Ong'ondo Were was a Kenyan politician who represented Kasipul Constituency in Homa Bay County during the 12th Parliament on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. He died while in office, becoming the second Member of Parliament for Kasipul to die in office, after Peter Owidi, who died in the evening of 23 November 2005.

Chirau Ali Mwakwere is a Kenyan politician and diplomat who represented Matuga Constituency in Kwale County during the 9th Parliament under the National Rainbow Coalition (NARC). He joined government service in 1967 and served as an ambassador to several countries as well as in domestic postings, including in education. A member of the Kenya African National Union until 2002, where he rose to deputy leader, he left to join NARC, which won the 2002 election. He served as Kenya's Foreign Minister from June 2004 to December 2005 and then as Transport Minister from December 2005, following a cabinet reshuffle prompted by tensions within NARC.

Christine Ombaka Oduor is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Siaya County across the 11th, 12th and 13th Parliaments on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. In the 13th Parliament she sits on the Education Committee, a Departmental Committee, and the Special Funds Accounts Committee.
Christopher Ndarathi Mūrūngarū is a Kenyan politician who represented Kieni Constituency in Nyeri County during the 9th Parliament under NARC. He served as Kenya's Minister of Transport.

Chrisantus Okemo is a Kenyan politician who represented Nambale Constituency in Busia County from the 1997 Kenyan parliamentary election, serving in the 9th Parliament, initially associated with the Kenya African National Union and later the Orange Democratic Movement.

Cornelly Serem is a Kenyan politician who represented Aldai Constituency in Nandi County, first elected on a United Republican Party ticket in the 11th Parliament and later under the Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament, where he served on a Departmental Committee.

Muge Cynthia Jepkosgei, popularly known as Cheptikonyol, is a Kenyan politician who serves as Woman Representative for Nandi County, elected in a landslide victory in 2022 to the 13th Parliament under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). She previously served in Kilibwoni and sits on the Committee on Implementation, a Departmental Committee, and the Health Committee.

Kubai Cyprian Iringo is a Kenyan politician and diplomat who represented Igembe Central Constituency in Meru County, first elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket in the 11th Parliament and re-elected under the Jubilee Party for the 12th Parliament, where he chaired the Constituencies Development Committee. In the 11th Parliament he served on the Finance, Planning and Trade Committee and spoke 462 times on the floor of the House. In December 2013, ODM threatened to expel him, alongside fellow MP Mpuru Aburi, for expressing willingness to work with the incoming Jubilee Coalition government. He received the Shujaa Parliamentarian Award in 2015 for championing citizens' interests in health matters, and was appointed Kenya's ambassador to Somalia in October 2023.

Daniel Kazungu Muzee, also known as Dan Kiili Karitho, is a Kenyan politician who represented Malindi Constituency in Kilifi County during the 11th Parliament, elected in 2013 on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket, before losing his re-election bid in 2017. He later represented Igembe Central Constituency in Meru County under the Jubilee Party in the 13th Parliament, serving on the Constitutional Implementation Oversight, Departmental and Public Investments Committees. He has also served as Cabinet Secretary in the Ministry of Mining.

Dan Kazungu Muzee is a Kenyan politician who represented Malindi Constituency, in Kilifi County, in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament under the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM), having been elected in 2013. He lost his bid for re-election in 2017. He later served as Cabinet Secretary in the Ministry of Mining.

Danson Buya Mungatana is a Kenyan lawyer and politician, popularly known as 'the crocodile eater,' who serves as Senator for Tana River County under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), sitting in the 13th Parliament. He belonged to NARC-Kenya and represented Garsen Constituency in the National Assembly from 2002 to 2013, winning the seat in both the 2002 and 2007 general elections, recorded here in the 9th Parliament. During his time as an MP he also served as Assistant Minister in various government ministries.

David (Daudi) Mwīraria is a Kenyan politician who represented North Imenti Constituency in the 9th Parliament. He served as Kenya's Minister for Finance and later as Minister for Environment and Natural Resources. Running for re-election in December 2007 on a Party of National Unity ticket, he was defeated by Silas Muriuki of the Mazingira Green Party of Kenya.

professional.title: Minister for Environment and Natural Resources

bio: David Eseli Simiyu is a Kenyan politician who represented Tongaren Constituency in Bungoma County across the 11th and 12th Parliaments under the Forum for Restoration of Democracy-Kenya (FORD-Kenya). He was first elected to represent the area, then known as Kimilili Constituency, in the 2007 Kenyan parliamentary election.

David Gikaria is a Kenyan politician who represents Nakuru Town East Constituency in Nakuru County. First elected in 2013 on a The National Alliance ticket, he was re-elected in 2017 under the Jubilee Party and again in 2022 under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), now serving a third term in the 13th Parliament. He sits on the Appointments, Energy, Liaison and Joint Committees, among other Departmental assignments in the National Assembly, having previously served on a Departmental Committee in the 12th Parliament as well.

David Karithi is a Kenyan politician who represented Tigania West Constituency in Meru County during the 11th Parliament, elected on a The National Alliance (TNA) ticket in 2013. He served on the House Committee on Health, and according to Mzalendo Trust made a total of 34 speeches on the floor of the House during the 2013 to 2016 session.

David Musila is a Kenyan politician who represented Mwingi South Constituency, elected in the 1997 Kenyan parliamentary election and recorded in the 9th Parliament under NARC. He later became associated with the Wiper Democratic Movement, and in 2013 he was elected as the first Senator representing Kitui County.

David Ouma Ochieng' is a Kenyan politician who represents Ugenya Constituency in Siaya County and leads the Movement for Democracy and Growth (MDG) party, which he has represented since the 12th Parliament after first being elected on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket in the 11th Parliament. In the 13th Parliament he serves on the Budget and Appropriations, Parliamentary Powers and Privileges, Procedure and House Rules, and Regional Integration Committees, alongside a Departmental Committee.

David Aoko Were is a Kenyan politician who has represented Matungu Constituency in Kakamega County since the 2007 Kenyan parliamentary election, recorded in the 9th Parliament under NARC and later the 11th Parliament under New Ford Kenya, where he served on Departmental and Public Investments Committees. He has also been associated with the Orange Democratic Movement.

The late Denar Joseph Hamisi was a Kenyan politician nominated to the National Assembly under Article 97(1)(c) in 2022 to represent the Amani National Congress (ANC) in the 13th Parliament. He served on the Departmental Committee on Sports and Culture and the Select Committee on National Cohesion and Equal Opportunities. He died on 6 December 2025 in a road crash near his home in Karen.

Dennitah Ghati is a Kenyan politician who served as Woman Representative for Migori County in the 11th Parliament and was later nominated to the National Assembly in the 12th Parliament to represent persons with disabilities, both terms on an Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket.

Col (Rtd) Ali Rasso Dido is a Kenyan politician who has represented Saku Constituency in Marsabit County since March 2013. He was first elected on a United Republican Party ticket for the 11th Parliament, where he served on a Departmental Committee, then re-elected under the Jubilee Party for the 12th Parliament, and again under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) for the 13th Parliament, where he sits on the Administration and Internal Security, Appointments, Joint and Departmental Committees.

Dorice Donya Aburi is a Kenyan politician who serves as Woman Representative for Kisii County under the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya (WDM-K) party, sitting in the 13th Parliament, where she serves on the Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers Committee, the Labour Committee, and a Departmental Committee.

Aden Bare Duale is a Kenyan politician who represented Garissa Township Constituency across the 11th, 12th and 13th Parliaments, moving from the United Republican Party to the Jubilee Party and then the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), and serving on the Departmental and Justice and Legal Affairs Committees in the 12th Parliament. He left Parliament to join the cabinet, serving as Cabinet Secretary for Defence from 31 October 2022 until his dismissal on 11 July 2024. He was reinstated eight days later as Cabinet Secretary for Environment, Climate Change and Forestry before being moved to the Ministry of Health, a post he has held since 2025. He hails from the Ogaden-AbudWaaq branch of the greater Darod clan.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Environment, Climate Change and Forestry

bio: Edith Vethi Nyenze is a Kamba Kenyan politician who represents Kitui West Constituency, elected to succeed her late husband Francis Nyenze as the seventh Member of Parliament for the seat. She belongs to the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya (WDM-K) and has served across the 12th and 13th Parliaments, sitting in the 13th on the Public Petitions, Social Protection and Departmental Committees.

Edwin Watenya Sifuna is a Kenyan politician who has served as Senator for Nairobi City County since the 2022 general election, representing the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) in the 13th Parliament, where he holds the position of deputy minority whip. He served as Secretary General of the ODM party from 2018 until 9 July 2026.

Ekwee David Ethuro is a Kenyan politician who represented Turkana Central Constituency in the National Assembly from 1998 to 2013, recorded here in the 9th Parliament under NARC. He served as Assistant Minister for Planning and National Development from 1998 to 2002 under President Daniel arap Moi, and after re-election in 2007 acted as Speaker of the House in the 10th Parliament whenever the Speaker and Deputy Speaker were absent. On 28 March 2013 he became the first Speaker of the modern Kenyan Senate. He currently chairs the Higher Education Loans Board (HELB).

professional.title: Assistant Minister for Planning and National Development

bio: Elias Bare Shill is a Kenyan politician who represented Fafi Constituency in Garissa County during the 11th Parliament on a United Republican Party (URP) ticket.

Elijah Kiptarbei Lagat is a Kenyan former marathon runner who represented Chesumei Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament on a United Republican Party ticket. Before his single term in politics, he built an international athletics reputation, most notably winning the 2000 Boston Marathon in the closest finish in the race's history, edging out Ethiopia's Gezahegne Abera and fellow Kenyan Moses Tanui after all three clocked around 2 hours 9 minutes. That victory extended Kenya's hold on the men's Boston Marathon title to a tenth consecutive year, a streak that still stands as a record. Lagat's fame as an athlete preceded and shaped his entry into elective office representing his home constituency of Chesumei.

Elijah Njoroge Kururia is a Kenyan politician who represents Gatundu North Constituency in the National Assembly, having won the seat as an Independent candidate without a party affiliation and taking his seat in the 13th Parliament. During his term he has sat on two departmental committees, Education and Public Investments, giving him a hand in shaping oversight of the education sector and state investment corporations. Beyond this legislative role, detailed public information about his earlier career, education, or professional background before entering politics remains limited, with his public profile centred almost entirely on his work representing Gatundu North in Parliament since the 2022 general election.

Elsie Busihile Muhanda is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Kakamega County since 2017, sitting for the Orange Democratic Movement across the 12th and 13th Parliaments. She was re-elected in 2022 in a landslide, garnering 300,426 votes, a margin that underscored her enduring popularity in the county. In the 13th Parliament she has served on the Members Service and Facilities, Sports and Culture, and Transport and Infrastructure committees, giving her oversight roles spanning parliamentary welfare, culture, and national infrastructure. Her two consecutive terms mark her as one of the more established Woman Representatives from western Kenya, continuing to advocate for Kakamega women in the National Assembly.

Joseph Gachoki Gitari is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kirinyaga Central Constituency in the National Assembly across two non-consecutive terms. He first won the seat in a February 2011 by-election following the departure of former MP Ngata Kariuki, sitting in the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket. He returned to Parliament in the 13th Parliament under the United Democratic Alliance, where he has served on the Departmental, Diaspora and Migrant Workers, Lands, and Regional Integration committees, continuing his long association with representing Kirinyaga Central.

Joseph Gachoki Gitari is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kirinyaga Central Constituency in the National Assembly since February 2011, first winning the seat after the removal of former MP Ngata Kariuki. He served in the 11th Parliament under TNA and later in the 13th Parliament under the United Democratic Alliance, where he sits on departmental committees covering Diaspora and Migrant Workers, Lands, and Regional Integration.

Enoch Kiio Wambua is a Kenyan politician serving as Senator for Kitui County, having been elected on a Wiper ticket to take up his seat in the 13th Parliament. As a member of the Senate, he represents Kitui at the national level, a role focused on legislation affecting counties and oversight of devolved government functions. No parliamentary committee assignments are recorded for him in this term, and detailed public information about his earlier career, education, or professional background before entering the Senate remains limited, with his public profile centred on his current role representing Kitui County in the upper house of Kenya's Parliament.

Karemba Eric Muchangi Njiru is a Kenyan politician representing Runyenjes Constituency in Embu County, first elected in 2017 and re-elected in 2022 on a United Democratic Alliance ticket for his second term in the 13th Parliament, having earlier sat under the Jubilee Party banner in the 12th Parliament. He chairs the National Assembly's Public Petitions Committee and has also served on the Constitutional Implementation Oversight, Departmental, Liaison, and Selection committees, reflecting a sustained role in overseeing House business through consecutive terms representing Runyenjes.

Esther Murugi Mathenge is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Nyeri Town Constituency, sitting in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket, part of the Jubilee coalition that governed Kenya at the time. No committee assignments are recorded for her in this term. She is now a former member of the National Assembly, and detailed public information about her career before or after this parliamentary term, including any professional or educational background, remains limited, with her public profile centred on her single term representing Nyeri Town in Nyeri County.

Eve Akinyi Obara is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kabondo Kasipul Constituency in Homa Bay County since first winning the seat in 2017, and was re-elected in 2022 for a second term in the 13th Parliament on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket. Before entering Parliament, she served as Managing Director of the Kenya Literature Bureau. In the current Parliament she sits on the Education and Special Funds Accounts committees, building on a legislative and public-administration career spanning her time managing a state corporation and now two terms representing Kabondo Kasipul.

Ezekiel Machogu Ombaki is a Kenyan politician who represented Nyaribari Masaba Constituency in the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament on a National Agenda Party of Kenya ticket, serving as a directly elected constituency MP in Kisii County. He later served as Cabinet Secretary for the Ministry of Education after being nominated by President William Ruto, taking charge of the national education docket at a time of significant policy reform. His career marks a clear progression from constituency-level legislative politics to a senior national executive role overseeing education policy and administration for the country, reflecting a shift from parliamentary representation to cabinet-level government service.

Fabian Kyule Muli is a Kenyan politician and businessman who has represented Kangundo Constituency in Machakos County since 2017, sitting in the 13th Parliament on the ticket of the Grand Dream Development Party (GDDP), of which he is party leader, having earlier run under the Muungano Party banner in the 12th Parliament. Before entering politics he served in the United States Army and later went into business. In his current term he sits on the Departmental, Labour, and Parliamentary Powers and Privileges committees, leading the sole parliamentary seat held by his party.

Fahim Yasin Twaha is a Kenyan politician who represented Lamu West Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket, serving as the area's elected representative in Nairobi. He went on to become the second governor of Lamu County, leading the county's devolved government until leaving office on 11 August 2022 at the end of his term. His career reflects a clear progression from national legislative representation of a coastal constituency to county-level executive leadership, overseeing devolved functions including infrastructure, health, and local development for Lamu County over an extended period in office.

Faith Wairimu Gitau is a Kenyan politician who has represented Nyandarua County as Woman Representative across two terms, sitting for the Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament before moving to the United Democratic Alliance in the 13th Parliament, reflecting the broader realignment of Jubilee-affiliated legislators after the 2022 general election. In her current term she serves on five committees: Departmental, House Business, Parliamentary Powers and Privileges, Social Protection, and Staff Welfare, giving her a wide-ranging role in both policy oversight and the internal administration of the National Assembly. This breadth of committee work, spanning two parliaments, reflects continuity in representing Nyandarua women through changing party alignments.

Farah Maalim Mohamed is a veteran Kenyan politician and Advocate of the High Court of Kenya who currently represents Dadaab Constituency in Garissa County, elected in August 2022 on a Wiper Democratic Movement ticket and serving in the 13th Parliament. He earlier represented Lagdera Constituency from 1992 to 1997 and again from 2007 to 2013, during which he served as Deputy Speaker of the National Assembly from 2008 to 2013. In his current term he is a member of the Speaker's Panel and sits on committees including Budget and Appropriations, Defence and Foreign Relations, Justice and Legal Affairs, and Public Investments, capping a decades-long parliamentary career spanning multiple constituencies and leadership positions.

professional.title: Advocate of the High Court of Kenya

bio: Fatuma Abdi Jehow is a Kenyan politician who serves as Woman Representative for Wajir County in the 13th Parliament, elected in the August 2022 general election on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket. She won the seat with 36,064 votes, defeating incumbent Fatuma Gedi of Jubilee in a closely watched contest for the county's Woman Representative position. In her current term she sits on the Lands and Public Investments committees, giving her oversight roles touching on land policy and state corporations. Her election marked her first term representing Wajir women in the National Assembly, unseating a sitting incumbent in the process.

Fatuma Ibrahim Ali is a Kenyan politician of Somali ethnicity who represented Wajir County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, having been elected in March 2013 on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket. She has served as a Commissioner of the Kenya National Commission on Human Rights, and from 2017 to 2022 she sat in the East African Legislative Assembly (EALA) as a member representing Kenya.

professional.title: Commissioner
professional.institution: Kenya National Commission on Human Rights

bio: Fatuma Zainab Mohammed is a Kenyan politician who serves as the Independent Woman Representative for Migori County in the National Assembly's 13th Parliament, a term running from August 2022 to 2027. She won the seat as an independent candidate, defeating Dennitah Ghati, having earlier represented Wajir South Constituency as MP on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament. She chairs the Special Funds Account Committee and also sits on the Departmental and Liaison committees, marking a shift from a party-affiliated constituency seat to an independently held Woman Representative seat in a different county.

Florence Chepngetich Koskey Bore is a Kenyan politician who was elected Woman Representative for Kericho County in the 2017 general election, sitting in the 12th Parliament on a Jubilee Party ticket. She later served as Cabinet Secretary for Labour and Social Protection from 2022, a position she lost on 11 July 2024 when President Ruto dismissed the entire cabinet, before which she was aligned with the United Democratic Alliance. Her career reflects a progression from county-level representation to a national executive appointment in the labour docket.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Labour and Social Protection
professional.institution: Ministry of Labour and Social Protection

bio: Florence Mwikali Mutua is a Kenyan politician from Busia town who served two terms as Woman Representative for Busia County, from August 2013 to August 2022, sitting in the 11th and 12th Parliaments on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket. In the 12th Parliament she served on the Departmental committee. In the 2022 general election she contested the Busia governorship but lost to Paul Otuoma, making her the only woman to vie for that seat that year; a win would have made her the county's first female governor.

Florence Jematiah Sergon is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Baringo County since the August 2022 general election, sitting in the 13th Parliament on a United Democratic Alliance ticket. She earlier served as a member of the East African Legislative Assembly (EALA) following her 2017 election to that regional body. In her current national role she sits on the Budget and Appropriations, Communication Information and Innovation, and Decentralised Funds Accounts committees, building on her earlier regional legislative experience.

Francis Chachu Ganya is a Kenyan politician who has represented North Horr Constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election, first on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket in the 11th Parliament and later under the Frontier Alliance in the 12th Parliament, reflecting a shift away from the larger national coalition toward a locally rooted political vehicle. During the 11th Parliament he served on the Departmental committee, giving him a role in legislative oversight alongside his constituency work. His sustained hold on the North Horr seat across multiple election cycles and shifting party affiliations reflects a long tenure representing this remote northern Kenyan constituency.

Francis Nyamu Kagwima is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Tharaka Constituency from 1988 to 1997 and was re-elected for the 2002 to 2007 term, sitting in the 9th Parliament on a FORD-Asili ticket during an era of multiparty transition in Kenyan politics. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term. He went on to become the second deputy governor of Tharaka Nithi County, elected on a Jubilee Party ticket as running mate to Onesmus Muthomi Njuki, marking a transition from national legislative office decades earlier to county executive leadership following the creation of devolved county government.

Francis Mwanzia Nyenze was a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Kitui West Constituency from 1997 to 2002 and again from 2013 to 2017, sitting in the 11th Parliament on a Wiper Democratic Movement ticket during his later term. He served as Minister for Environment from 1997 to 2001 and Minister for Heritage and Sports from 2001 to 2002, and later served as Leader of Minority in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament from 2013 to 2017. He died at The Nairobi Hospital after a long battle with colon cancer.

Gabriel Gathuka Kagombe is a Kenyan politician from the United Democratic Alliance who was elected in the 2022 general election to succeed Moses Kuria as Member of Parliament for Gatundu South Constituency, serving in the 13th Parliament. He holds a political science degree from the University of Nairobi, an academic background in governance and public affairs that preceded his entry into elective politics. In his current term he sits on the Agriculture and Livestock, Departmental, and Public Accounts committees, giving him oversight roles spanning agricultural policy and public spending. This marks his first term representing Gatundu South, succeeding a high-profile predecessor in the seat.

Peninnah Gathoni Wamuchomba, also known as Gathoni Wa Muchomba, is a Kenyan journalist, entrepreneur, and politician who has represented Githunguri Constituency in Kiambu County since the 2022 general election, sitting in the 13th Parliament as a member of the ruling United Democratic Alliance. She previously served as Kiambu County Woman Representative from 2017 to 2022 on a Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament. In her current term she sits on the Constitution Implementation Oversight, Finance and National Planning, and Members Service and Facilities committees, combining a media and business background with sustained representation of Kiambu-area constituents.

Nixon Kiprotich Korir is a Kenyan politician who represented Langata Constituency in the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament on a Jubilee Party ticket, serving as the elected representative for one of Nairobi's constituencies. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term. He later served as the nominated Principal Secretary for the State Department for Lands and Physical Planning, a senior appointed administrative post overseeing national land policy, marking a shift from elected legislative office to an appointed executive role within the national government bureaucracy responsible for land administration.

professional.title: Principal Secretary
professional.institution: State Department for Lands and Physical Planning

bio: George Muchai was a Kenyan politician and trade unionist who served as a member of the National Assembly following the 2013 elections, sitting in the 11th Parliament as a member of the Jubilee Alliance under the TNA party ticket, without a specified constituency recorded for this term. No committee assignments are recorded for him during his time in Parliament. His public career combined longstanding leadership within the trade union movement with a single term representing his constituents in the National Assembly, reflecting a background rooted in labour organising before his move into elective national politics.

George Musengi Saitoti was a Kenyan politician, businessman, and American- and British-trained economist, mathematician, and development policy thinker who represented Kajiado North Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a NARC ticket, sitting in Parliament during the early years of the NARC government. No committee assignments are recorded for this term. He combined an academic background in economics and mathematics gained at institutions in the United States and United Kingdom with a long career in Kenyan politics and business, giving him a reputation as one of the country's more intellectually distinguished public figures of his generation.

education.title: Studies in Economics and Mathematics
education.institution: Universities in the United States and United Kingdom

bio: Gertrude Mbeyu Mwanyanje is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Kilifi County since being first elected in 2017, sitting for the Orange Democratic Movement across the 12th and 13th Parliaments. She won her 2022 re-election with 111,039 votes, or 39.33 percent of the vote, in a contest that returned her to the National Assembly for a second term. In her current term she sits on the Decentralized Funds Accounts and Environment, Forestry and Mining committees, giving her a role overseeing devolved development funds and environmental policy, reflecting her sustained tenure representing Kilifi women in Parliament across consecutive election cycles.

Maitha Gideon Mung'aro is a Kenyan politician who has served as Governor of Kilifi County since August 2022. He was elected to represent Kilifi North Constituency in the National Assembly in the 2013 election, sitting in the 11th Parliament as a member of the Coalition for Reforms and Democracy and the Orange Democratic Movement. During his parliamentary term he served as chief whip, succeeding Jakoyo Midiwo, chaired the African Union Parliamentary Group, and sat on the House Business, Selection, and Lands committees. He earlier served as mayor of Malindi and represented Malindi Constituency before it was split, giving him a career spanning local government, national legislature, and county executive leadership.

Gideon Musyoka Ndambuki is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Kaiti Constituency from its creation in 1997 until 2013, sitting in the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket before switching to the Orange Democratic Movement-Kenya for the 2007 election. He also served as a government minister between 1998 and 2002. He unsuccessfully sought the Wiper Democratic Party nomination for the Makueni senate seat ahead of the 2013 general election. Educated in the United States, he worked at the Commercial Bank of Africa in the 1980s and later served as Managing Director of Trade Bank between 1986 and its collapse in 1993.

Gideon Mwiti Irea was a Kenyan politician who served as a Member of Parliament in Kenya, representing Central Imenti Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament on an Alliance Party of Kenya ticket. No committee assignments are recorded for him during this parliamentary term. Public information about his career before or after his time in Parliament remains limited, with his public profile centred almost entirely on his single term representing the Imenti Central area of Meru County in the National Assembly of Kenya.

Gladwell Jesire Cheruiyot is a Kenyan politician who served as Woman Representative for Baringo County, sitting in the 12th Parliament and serving on the Departmental committee, her only recorded committee assignment during this term in the National Assembly. She has been a member of the Kenya African National Union since 2017, representing continuity with the party in her post-parliamentary political affiliation. Her single term as Woman Representative marks the primary public record of her political career, centred on representing Baringo County women in the National Assembly.

Gladys Atieno Wanga is a Kenyan politician who serves as Governor of Homa Bay County, the first female governor from the western region of the country and one of seven women elected governor in the 2022 general election. She previously served as Woman Representative for Homa Bay County from 2013 to 2022, sitting in the 11th and 12th Parliaments on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket. She is married to George Wanga, former non-executive chairperson of the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA), and her career reflects a progression from two terms in the National Assembly to county executive leadership.

Gladys Atieno Wanga is a Kenyan politician who serves as Governor of Homa Bay County, the first female governor from the western region of the country and one of seven women elected governor in the 2022 general election. She previously served as Woman Representative for Homa Bay County, including a term in the 11th Parliament on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket, having held the seat from 2013 to 2022. She is married to George Wanga, former non-executive chairperson of the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA), reflecting a career that moved from national legislative representation to county executive office.

Gladys Jepkosgei Boss Shollei is a Kenyan politician who serves as Deputy Speaker of the National Assembly and Woman Representative for Uasin Gishu County, sitting in the 13th Parliament as a member of the United Democratic Alliance. In her current term she sits on four committees, Appointments, Justice and Legal Affairs, Liaison, and Procedure and House Rules, giving her a central role in the House's internal governance alongside her constituency representation. This combination of an elected Woman Representative seat with the senior presiding office of Deputy Speaker places her among the more prominent leadership figures in the current National Assembly.

Godfrey Gitahi Kariuki, known as GG Kariuki, was a Kenyan politician who entered elective politics at independence in 1963. He served as Senator for Laikipia County in the 11th Parliament from 2013 until his death on 30 June 2017, and was one of the only Kenyan politicians to serve under four presidents. He previously served several terms as Member of Parliament for Laikipia West Constituency, sitting in the 9th Parliament on a NARC ticket, and served as a government minister under both Presidents Jomo Kenyatta and Daniel arap Moi.

professional.title: Cabinet Minister
professional.institution: Governments of Jomo Kenyatta and Daniel arap Moi

bio: Grace Jelagat Kimoi Kipchoim was a Kenyan politician who represented Baringo South Constituency in the National Assembly, sitting in the 11th Parliament on a United Republican Party ticket and later the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party as a coalition member of the Jubilee Alliance. She won her seat while ill, campaigning with limited mobility, narrowly defeating challenger Charles Kamuren of KANU by 9,299 votes to 8,905. Rumours of her death circulated on social media in early 2018, which she publicly denied as detractors' attempts to tarnish her name, before she died of cancer on 20 April 2018 while under treatment at Nairobi Hospital.

Harrison Mwalimu Garama Kombe is a Kenyan teacher and politician who has represented Magarini Constituency in the National Assembly across multiple non-consecutive terms. He was first elected in 2002 on a Shirikisho Party ticket, sitting in the 9th Parliament, lost the seat in 2007, and returned in 2013, being re-elected in 2017 before losing again in the 2022 election while serving in the 11th and 13th Parliaments on a United Republican Party ticket and later for Orange Democratic Movement respectively. In his most recent term he served on the Budget and Appropriations committee, capping a career combining teaching with several stints representing Magarini.

Hassan Abdi Dukicha is a Kenyan politician who represented Galole Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected on a United Democratic Forum ticket with the support of the Amani Coalition in the 2013 general election. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term. He later ran for the Tana River senate seat in the 2022 general election but lost to the incumbent, marking an unsuccessful bid to move from constituency-level representation to a county-wide Senate seat after having left the National Assembly at the end of his single term.

Hassan Mohamed Mwanyoha is a Kenyan politician who represented Matuga Constituency, in Kwale County, in the National Assembly, first elected in 2002 and again in 2013 under the Orange Democratic Movement. Before entering national politics, he served for 15 years as a councilor for Tiwi Ward on a Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket.

Henry Kiprono Kosgey is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Tinderet Constituency, sitting in the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket, and became known as the longest-serving MP for that constituency over the course of his political career. No committee assignments are recorded for this term. He served as Minister for Industrialization and was Chairman of the Orange Democratic Movement, and is also recognised as a renowned large-scale tea farm owner, combining a long agribusiness career with senior party leadership and cabinet-level government service.

Hezron Awiti Bollo is a Kenyan businessman and politician who represented Nyali Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected on a Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket in the 2013 general election. No committee assignments are recorded for him during this parliamentary term. Detailed public information about his business ventures and broader career remains limited beyond his single term in Parliament, with his profile centred on combining a business background with his role representing Nyali Constituency in Mombasa County during his time in the National Assembly.

Mohamed Yusuf Haji was a Kenyan politician who served as Minister of State for Defence from 2008 to 2013 and briefly as acting Minister of Internal Security and Provincial Affairs in 2012. He represented Ijara Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket, and later represented Mandera West as MP in the 12th and 13th Parliaments under the Economic Freedom Party and United Democratic Movement respectively, sitting on the Departmental, Liaison, National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity, and Select committees in his later terms. He also served in the Senate of Kenya from 2013 under the Jubilee party before his death on 15 February 2021.

professional.title: Acting Minister of Internal Security and Provincial Affairs

bio: Mohamed Yussuf Haji was a Kenyan politician who represented Ijara Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament under KANU. He went on to serve as Minister of State for Defence from 2008 to 2013 and briefly as acting Minister of Internal Security and Provincial Affairs in 2012. From 2013 he served in the Senate of Kenya under the Jubilee Party. He died on 15 February 2021.

professional.title: Acting Minister of Internal Security and Provincial Affairs

bio: Charity Kathambi Chepkwony is a Kenyan politician serving as Member of Parliament for Njoro Constituency in Nakuru County, first elected in 2017 and re-elected in 2022, now serving her second term in the 13th Parliament on a United Democratic Alliance ticket after earlier sitting under the Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament. In her current term she sits on the Committee on Implementation, Departmental, Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers, and Environment, Forestry and Mining committees, reflecting sustained representation of Njoro across two parliamentary terms.

Danson Mwashako Mwakuwona is a Kenyan economist and politician who has represented Wundanyi Constituency in Taita Taveta County since first being elected in 2017, re-elected in 2022 for a second term in the 13th Parliament on a Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket, having sat under the same party in the 12th Parliament. In his current term he serves on the Budget and Appropriations and Regional Integration committees, roles that draw on his economics background in overseeing national fiscal policy and cross-border regional cooperation. His two consecutive terms reflect sustained voter support for his continued representation of Wundanyi in the National Assembly.

Janet Jepkemboi Sitienei is a Kenyan politician who represents Turbo Constituency in Uasin Gishu County, first winning the seat as an independent candidate in 2017 while sitting in the 12th Parliament, then again in 2022 on a United Democratic Alliance ticket for the 13th Parliament. She serves as Vice-Chairperson of the National Assembly's Public Petitions Committee and also sits on the Sports and Culture committee in her current term, while having sat on the Departmental committee in the 12th Parliament. In 2024 she was honoured as a Chief of the Order of the Burning Spear by President Ruto, recognising her public service.

Josephine Naisula Lesuuda is a Kenyan politician and women's rights activist who represents Samburu West Constituency in the National Assembly, elected on a Kenya African National Union ticket and serving her second term in the 13th Parliament after re-election in 2022, having also sat for the same party in the 12th Parliament. She previously served as a nominated Senator in 2013. She chairs the National Assembly's Regional Integration Committee and, in earlier terms, was a member of the East Africa Legislative Assembly (EALA) delegation and Select committees, combining activism for women's rights with a legislative career spanning both chambers of Parliament.

Ndindi Nyoro is a Kenyan politician, businessman, and entrepreneur who represents Kiharu Constituency in Murang'a County, sitting in the 13th Parliament on a United Democratic Alliance ticket after earlier serving under the Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament. He is the immediate former Chair of the Budget and Appropriations Committee and has also served on the Departmental, Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers, Mediation, and Select committees, and has announced his departure from UDA with his current party affiliation unsettled. A student leader at Kenyatta University, he holds a Bachelor of Arts in Economics from the same institution and pursued two master's degrees concurrently, an MA in Economics from the University of Nairobi and an MSc in Economics from Kenyatta University, both completed in 2025.

Sarah Paulata Korere is a Kenyan politician who represents Laikipia North Constituency in Laikipia County, winning the seat in the 2022 general election with 14,148 votes to serve in the 13th Parliament on a Jubilee Party ticket, having also held the seat under the same party in the 12th Parliament. She earlier served as a nominated Member of Parliament representing a national constituency in the 11th Parliament on a United Republican Party ticket. In her current term she sits on the Administration and Internal Security, House Business, and Public Investments committees, reflecting a parliamentary career spanning both nominated and directly elected seats.

Ibrahim Abdi Saney is a Kenyan politician who represents Wajir North Constituency in Wajir County, sitting in the 13th Parliament on a United Democratic Alliance ticket, after earlier representing Wajir North in the 11th Parliament for the Orange Democratic Movement and Lafey Constituency in the 12th Parliament under the Economic Freedom Party. His 2022 election was challenged by petition but upheld, with his 6,502 votes standing. In his current term he sits on the Departmental, Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers, and Transport and Infrastructure committees, and previously served on the Departmental committee in the 12th Parliament, reflecting a legislative career across different constituencies and parties.

Innocent Mugabe Maino is a Kenyan politician from the Orange Democratic Movement who represents Likuyani Constituency in the National Assembly, serving in the 13th Parliament following his election in the 2022 general election. In his current term he sits on three committees, Delegated Legislation, Departmental, and Tourism and Wildlife, giving him a role in reviewing subsidiary legislation alongside sector-specific oversight of tourism and wildlife policy. This marks his first term representing Likuyani in the National Assembly, with his public profile centred on his current legislative role.

Innocent Momanyi Obiri is a Kenyan politician who has represented constituencies in Kisii County across three parliaments: Borabu on a Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket in the 11th Parliament, Bobasi for the Peoples Democratic Party in the 12th Parliament, and Bobasi again for Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya in the 13th Parliament. In his current term he chairs the Decentralised Funds Accounts committee and also sits on the Departmental and Housing, Urban Planning and Public Works committees, reflecting a long-running legislative career across shifting constituency boundaries and party affiliations.

Irene Muthoni Kasalu is a Kenyan politician who has served as Woman Representative for Kitui County since being first elected in 2017, re-elected in 2022 with 201,899 votes to defeat Peninah Malonza of UDA, sitting for the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya across the 12th and 13th Parliaments. She worked as a lecturer before entering politics. In her current term she sits on the Defence, Intelligence and Foreign Relations, Departmental, and Public Debt and Privatisation committees, combining an academic background with sustained representation of Kitui women.

Irene Njoki Mrembo is a Kenyan politician who was elected Member of Parliament for Bahati Constituency in Nakuru County on 9 August 2022, serving in the 13th Parliament on a Jubilee Party ticket. Before entering elective politics, she was personal assistant to James Macharia, then Cabinet Secretary for Transport, Infrastructure, Housing, Urban Development and Public Works, Shipping and Maritime, during his 2017 to 2022 tenure. In her current term she sits on the Lands, Public Debt and Privatisation, and Regional Integration committees, marking a transition from a government advisory role to elected office.

professional.title: Personal Assistant to the Cabinet Secretary
professional.institution: Ministry of Transport, Infrastructure, Housing, Urban Development and Public Works

bio: Irshadali Mohamed Sumra is a Kenyan businessman and politician who was elected Member of Parliament for Embakasi South Constituency in the 2013 parliamentary elections, sitting in the 11th Parliament on an Orange Democratic Movement ticket representing a densely populated constituency in Nairobi County. No committee assignments are recorded for him during this term. His public profile combines a business background with a single term representing Embakasi South in the National Assembly, with limited further public information available about his career beyond this period in elective politics.

Francis Irungu Kang'ata is a Kenyan politician and lawyer who serves as Governor of Murang'a County, having earlier served as Senator for the same county, Senate Majority Chief Whip, Member of Parliament for Kiharu Constituency in the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket, and Councillor for Central Ward in Murang'a. He holds a law degree from the University of Nairobi and a PhD in Law from the same institution, and teaches law at the Catholic University of Eastern Africa in Nairobi. He is also a principal in the law firm Irungu Kang'ata & Co. Advocates, reflecting a career spanning legal practice, academia, and progressively senior elected office at constituency, county Senate, and county executive levels.

Mwandawiro Mghanga is a Kenyan politician who represented Wundanyi Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a FORD-People ticket, sitting as an elected representative for this Taita Taveta constituency. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term. He went on to serve as chairperson of the Communist Party of Kenya, reflecting a political trajectory from mainstream parliamentary representation in the 1990s toward ideological party leadership in later years, positioning him among Kenya's more left-leaning political figures.

Jackson Kiplagat Arap Mandago is a Kenyan politician who serves as Senator for Uasin Gishu County, sitting in the 13th Parliament on a UDA ticket. He previously served as the first Governor of Uasin Gishu County from 2013 to 2022, first elected in 2013 and re-elected in 2017 for his second and final term, and earlier represented Keiyo South Constituency as MP in the 11th Parliament on a United Republican Party ticket. His career reflects a progression from constituency MP to two terms as county governor and, most recently, to the Senate.

James Mwangi Gakuya is a Kenyan politician who has represented Embakasi North Constituency in Nairobi County since first winning the seat in the 2013 general election on a The National Alliance ticket in the 11th Parliament, and has been re-elected in subsequent elections, sitting for Jubilee Party in the 12th Parliament and United Democratic Alliance in the 13th Parliament. In his current term he sits on the Decentralised Funds Accounts, Departmental, and Parliamentary Broadcasting and Library committees, reflecting three consecutive terms representing Embakasi North.

James Njenga Karume was a Kenyan businessman and politician, born in Elementaita, Nakuru District, who represented Kiambaa Constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket, sitting as the elected representative for this Kiambu County constituency. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term. His public profile combines a business background with his parliamentary service representing Kiambaa, reflecting the pattern common among his generation of Kenyan politicians of combining private commercial interests with elective national office.

James Omingo Magara is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for South Mugirango Constituency from 2000 to 2009, sitting in the 9th Parliament. He first won the seat in a 2001 by-election on a Ford-Kenya ticket, succeeding his late brother Enoch Nyakieya Magara, retained it in 2002 under Ford-People and in 2007 under the Orange Democratic Movement, and served as Assistant Minister of Trade until the High Court nullified the South Mugirango seat in December 2009 over election irregularities, without finding him personally guilty of rigging. He contested the resulting 2010 by-election for the newly formed People's Democratic Party, of which he became party leader, but lost to Manson Nyamweya of Ford-People. In 2013 he ran for the Kisii senate seat, finishing third behind winner Chris Obure.

James Opiyo Wandayi is a Kenyan agricultural and development economist and politician who currently serves as Cabinet Secretary for Energy and Petroleum. He represented Ugunja Constituency in the National Assembly across three consecutive terms, sitting for the Orange Democratic Movement in the 11th and 12th Parliaments and later ODM again in the 13th Parliament, and served on the Departmental committee during the 11th Parliament. He holds honours including the Moran of the Order of the Golden Heart and the Chief of the Order of the Burning Spear, reflecting a career that moved from long-serving constituency MP to national cabinet office in the energy sector.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Energy and Petroleum

bio: Jamleck Irungu Kamau, also known as "Kabisa Kabisa," was a Kenyan politician who represented Kigumo Constituency in Murang'a County from 2007 to 2017, sitting in the 11th Parliament on a The National Alliance ticket as part of the Jubilee coalition. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term. He was the son of Kamau Muchunu from Kigumo, and his public profile centres on his decade representing Kigumo in the National Assembly, a tenure that spanned two full parliamentary terms in Central Kenya's Murang'a County.

Jane Jepkorir Kiptoo Chebaibai is a Kenyan politician who served as Woman Representative for Elgeyo Marakwet County in the National Assembly, sitting in the 12th Parliament on a Jubilee Party ticket. No committee assignments are recorded for this parliamentary term.
