He was first elected to the National Assembly representing Runyenjes Constituency in 1992, before winning the newly created Manyatta Constituency seat between 1998 and 2001, and then representing Manyatta again from 2002 to 2007 on a Party of National Unity ticket, in the area that now falls within Embu County. He went on to serve as Minister for Co-operative Development and Marketing, and later became the Senator for Embu County, where he chaired the Senate's Agriculture and Livestock Committee. His long record across the National Assembly, the cabinet and the Senate makes him one of Embu's most experienced political figures.

professional.title: Minister for Co-operative Development and Marketing

bio: Peter Njuguna Gitau is a Kenyan politician who has represented Mwea constituency in Kirinyaga County in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election. He was first elected under the Party of National Unity and later contested under The National Alliance, retaining the Mwea seat into the 11th Parliament. His tenure reflects the political realignments common among central Kenya legislators over that period, as many lawmakers shifted party affiliation between successive elections. He continued to serve Mwea's constituents through these changes in party alignment.

Peter Ochieng Orero, also known by his birth name Peter Ochieng Odoyo, is a Kenyan politician and economist whose career bridges two very different parliamentary eras. He was first elected to represent Nyakach constituency in the National Assembly in 1997, serving until 2007 under the National Rainbow Coalition banner, during which time he was Vice Chair of the Finance, Trade and Planning Committee. Between 2001 and 2005 he held the positions of Assistant Minister for Foreign Affairs, Assistant Minister for Labour, and Assistant Minister for Regional Development. He also spent years as Kenya and East Africa's coordinator for the World e-Parliament initiative. Decades later he returned to the National Assembly representing Kibra constituency on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket in the 13th Parliament, where he sits on the Education and National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity committees.

Peter Ochieng Odoyo is a Kenyan politician and economist who represented Nyakach Constituency in the National Assembly from 1997 to 2007 under NARC. Within Parliament he served as Vice Chair of the Finance, Trade and Planning Committee from 1998 to 2001, before holding the positions of Assistant Minister for Foreign Affairs, Assistant Minister for Labour, and Assistant Minister for Regional Development between 2001 and 2005. He has also been a longtime coordinator for the World e-Parliament in Kenya and East Africa.

Peter Safari Shehe is a Kenyan politician who represented Ganze constituency in Kilifi County in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, having been elected in the 2013 general election. He lost his bid for re-election in 2017, ending his single term as a national legislator. He remained active in local politics, going on to contest a lower-level position in Jaribuni ward during the 2022 general election. His political career reflects a shift from national to grassroots-level politics following his exit from Parliament.

Peter Kalerwa Salasya, popularly known as PK, is a Kenyan politician who has represented Mumias East constituency in Kakamega County in the National Assembly since the 2022 general election, on the Democratic Action Party-Kenya (DAP-K) ticket. Before entering politics he worked in the banking sector and as a tutor. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Members Service and Facilities and Parliamentary Broadcasting and Library committees. He is known for his grassroots-style campaigning and his vocal push to revive the Mumias Sugar Company, though his time in office has also been marked by controversy over assault and hate speech allegations against him.

Phylis Jepkemoi Bartoo is a Kenyan politician who has represented Moiben constituency in Uasin Gishu County in the National Assembly since the 2022 general election, elected on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. In the 13th Parliament she sits on the Departmental, Education, Procedure and House Rules, and Public Petitions committees, giving her a broad legislative workload spanning several distinct policy areas. Her election marked her entry into national politics, and she has continued to represent Moiben's constituents throughout the current term of Parliament, building a record as a first-time lawmaker from Uasin Gishu County.

Peter Anyang' Nyong'o is a Kenyan politician and author who has held office at nearly every level of Kenya's political system. He was elected to the National Assembly in December 2007, representing Kisumu Rural constituency under the National Rainbow Coalition banner, and served as acting leader of the Orange Democratic Movement between March and May 2009 while Raila Odinga was in the United States, having previously been the party's Secretary-General. In government he served as Minister for Medical Services and earlier as Minister for Planning and National Development. He later represented Kisumu as Senator from 2013 to 2017, before being elected Governor of Kisumu County, the position in which he has continued to serve.

professional.title: Minister for Planning and National Development

bio: Protus Ewesit Akuja, popularly known as Dr. Akujah, is a Kenyan politician holding a doctorate who has represented Loima constituency in Turkana County in the National Assembly across two non-consecutive terms. He was first elected in the 11th Parliament on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket and returned to the seat in the 13th Parliament under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). In his current term he sits on the Administration and Internal Security, Budget and Appropriations, Departmental, and National Government Constituencies Development committees, reflecting a broad role in scrutinizing security, finance and constituency development matters.

Purity Wangui Ngirici is a Kenyan politician who served as the second Woman Representative for Kirinyaga County, taking office in August 2017 following the general election of that year. Elected on the Jubilee Party ticket, she succeeded Winnie Karimi Njuguna as the county's representative for women in the National Assembly. During her term in the 12th Parliament she sat on a Departmental committee, contributing to legislative oversight in that area. Her single term placed her among the second cohort of Kenya's directly elected women representatives created under the 2010 constitution.

Rachael Kaki Nyamai is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kitui South constituency in the National Assembly across three parliamentary terms since 2013. She was first elected under the National Rainbow Coalition banner in the 11th Parliament, before moving to the Jubilee Party for her subsequent terms in the 12th and 13th Parliaments, the latter running from August 2022 to 2027. Over her tenure she has served on Departmental committees, and in the current Parliament she also sits on the Lands and Procedure and House Rules committees. Her sustained re-election reflects continued support from Kitui South voters across more than a decade in national politics.

Rachel Wambui Shebesh is a Kenyan politician who made history as the first Woman Representative for Nairobi County, serving from 2013 to 2017 in the 11th Parliament on The National Alliance ticket. After her term as an elected legislator, she went on to serve as a Chief Administrative Secretary in the Kenyan government, extending her public service career beyond the National Assembly. She is married to Frank Shebesh, and the couple marked the completion of their traditional marriage rites with a wedding ceremony in Githunguri in October 2021. Her single term representing Nairobi's women remains a notable milestone in the county's political history.

Rahab Mukami Wachira is a Kenyan politician who has served as the Woman Representative for Nyeri County in the National Assembly since 2017, first elected on the Jubilee Party ticket and re-elected in 2022 under the United Democratic Alliance (UDA), part of the Kenya Kwanza coalition, with roughly 88 percent of the vote. In the 13th Parliament she sits on the Appointments, National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity, Labour, and Public Debt and Privatization committees, among others. She is also a member of the Pan-African Parliament, where in May 2026 she was elected President of its Women's Caucus, extending her legislative work to the continental stage alongside her national role representing Nyeri's women.

Raila Amolo Odinga was a Kenyan politician who represented Langata constituency in the National Assembly from 1992 to 2013, including his time in the 9th Parliament under the National Rainbow Coalition banner. He served as Kenya's Prime Minister from 2008 to 2013, one of the most prominent roles of a career spanning more than two decades in Parliament. He later led the Azimio la Umoja-One Kenya Coalition Party and stood for the presidency five times without success, alleging electoral fraud on each occasion. His decades-long tenure as Langata's MP and his time as premier made him one of the defining figures of modern Kenyan politics.

professional.title: Party Leader
professional.institution: Azimio la Umoja-One Kenya Coalition Party

bio: Raphael Lentimalo is a Kenyan politician who has represented Samburu East constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election. He was affiliated with the Orange Democratic Movement earlier in his political career before later contesting under The National Alliance during the 11th Parliament. His continued re-election across these years reflects sustained support from Samburu East's electorate through shifting party alignments common among Rift Valley legislators of that period. His long tenure representing the constituency spans multiple electoral cycles and changes in the region's dominant political coalitions.

Raphael Tuju, EGH (Elder of the Golden Heart), is a Kenyan politician and former media professional who built a public profile long before entering politics. He worked as a journalist, television producer and communications consultant, and became the first African TV producer to win an International Emmy Award, for a documentary on health and development. He began his political career in 2002, when he was elected Member of Parliament for Rarieda constituency in Siaya County, serving during the 9th Parliament under the National Rainbow Coalition banner. His transition from an award-winning media career to elective office marked one of the more distinctive paths into Kenyan politics of his generation.

Rashid Bezimba is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kisauni constituency in Mombasa County in the National Assembly across non-consecutive terms. He was first elected in 2013 on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket as part of the CORD coalition, serving in the 11th Parliament, but lost his bid for re-election in 2017. He returned to the seat by winning the 2022 general election, marking a comeback after a five-year absence from the National Assembly. His political trajectory reflects the shifting fortunes of coastal ODM politicians over the past decade.

Reuben Kiborek is a Kenyan politician serving his first term as Member of Parliament for Mogotio constituency in Baringo County, having been elected in the 2022 general election on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. He won the seat with 17,364 votes, defeating opponents Dr. Daniel Tuitoek and Professor Sambili. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Decentralized Funds Accounts, Departmental, and Health committees, giving him a role spanning public finance oversight and health policy. His election marked his entry into national politics representing Mogotio's constituents in the current Parliament.

Richard Makenga Katemi is a Kenyan businessman and politician who represented Kaiti constituency in Makueni County in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected on the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket. Alongside his legislative duties he served on a Departmental committee. His background in business informed his entry into elective politics, and his single term in office marked his transition from the private sector into representing Kaiti's constituents in the National Assembly. His combined identity as both businessman and lawmaker was a defining feature of his public profile during his time in Parliament.

Richard Onyonka is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kisii County constituents in Parliament across three terms and two different chambers. He was first elected to the National Assembly in 2007 to represent Kitutu Chache South, initially aligned with the People's Democratic Party, before moving through the Orange Democratic Movement and later the Forum for Restoration of Democracy-Kenya, retaining the seat in the August 2017 election. During the 11th Parliament he served on the Budget and Appropriations committee. He later moved to the Senate, winning the Kisii County senatorial seat in the 13th Parliament on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket, extending his legislative career from the National Assembly into the upper chamber.

Rigathi Gachagua is a Kenyan politician who represented Mathira constituency in Nyeri County in the National Assembly from 2017 to 2022 on the Jubilee Party ticket, serving through the 12th Parliament. He left the National Assembly to become Kenya's second Deputy President, taking office on 13 September 2022, a position he held until his impeachment in October 2024. After leaving the deputy presidency he launched his own political outfit, the Democracy for the Citizens (DCP) Party, in May 2025, becoming its party leader. His career thus moved from a single term as a backbench MP to the country's second-highest office and then into leading a new political movement.

Roba Sharu Duba is a Kenyan politician who represented Moyale constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected in 2013 on the United Democratic Forum ticket. Before entering politics he had a long career in local government administration, serving as Chief Executive Officer of the Local Authorities Provident Fund and as town clerk in Moyale, Kilifi, Mombasa and Nairobi, resigning from that service to contest the Moyale parliamentary seat. As a legislator he focused heavily on security and inter-communal peacebuilding, particularly within Moyale, an area that has long grappled with cross-border and ethnic tensions.

Robert Gichimu Githinji is a Kenyan lawyer and politician who has represented Gichugu constituency in Kirinyaga County in the National Assembly since 2017. He was first elected on the Jubilee Party ticket in the 12th Parliament and was re-elected in 2022 on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket for the 13th Parliament, running through 2027. In his current term he sits on the Delegated Legislation, Departmental, and Trade, Industry and Cooperatives committees. His legal background has informed his work in the National Assembly, where he has built a record of consecutive re-election representing Gichugu's constituents.

Robert Mbui, CBS (Chief of the Order of the Burning Spear), is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kathiani constituency in Machakos County in the National Assembly across three consecutive terms since 2013, each time on the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket. He is the fifth person to hold the Kathiani seat since the constituency's creation in 1988. In the 13th Parliament he serves as Deputy Leader of Minority in the National Assembly, alongside sitting on the Appointments, Budget and Appropriations, Delegated Legislation, Departmental, House Business, National Government Constituencies Development Fund, and Public Petitions committees. He holds a Master's degree in Strategic Management from the Kenya Institute of Management.

Robert Pukose is a Kenyan politician and trained medical doctor who has represented Endebess constituency in Trans Nzoia County in the National Assembly since 2013, when he became the area's first MP following its creation under the 2010 Constitution. He won the seat that year on a United Republican Party ticket, later moving through the Jubilee Party and now the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) across three consecutive terms. A qualified consultant general surgeon by profession, he has combined his medical background with legislative work, serving as Vice Chairperson of the Budget and Appropriations Committee and Vice Chair of the Health Committee in the 13th Parliament, alongside seats on the Departmental and Selection committees.

Robinson Njeru Githae is a Kenyan politician who represented Ndia constituency in the National Assembly following the 2002 general election, aligned with the Party of National Unity. He held several assistant ministerial posts, covering justice, transport, and local government, before being elevated to full minister for Nairobi Metropolitan and later for finance. President Uhuru Kenyatta appointed him Kenya's ambassador to the United States on 14 August 2014, a posting he held before being transferred to Vienna in May 2019 to serve as ambassador to Austria, a role he continues to hold. His career thus moved from a single term in the National Assembly into an extended run of cabinet and diplomatic postings.

Rose Museo Mumo is a Kenyan politician who has served as the Woman Representative for Makueni County in the National Assembly since 2013, across three consecutive terms on the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket. She was re-elected in 2017 and again in 2022, the latter with 116,599 votes representing about 49 percent of the vote, for a term running from August 2022 to 2027. In the 13th Parliament she sits on the Implementation, Parliamentary Powers and Privileges, and Select committees. Her decade-plus tenure representing Makueni's women reflects sustained voter support across three general elections.

Roselinda Soipan Tuya is a Kenyan politician who represented Narok County as Woman Representative in the National Assembly across the 11th and 12th Parliaments, first elected on the United Republican Party ticket and later on the Jubilee Party ticket. During her time in the 11th Parliament she served on the Departmental and Select committees. She later moved from Parliament into the executive, serving as Cabinet Secretary for Environment, Climate Change and Forestry before being sworn in as Cabinet Secretary for Defence by President William Ruto on 8 August 2024, a position she continues to hold.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Environment, Climate Change and Forestry

bio: Rozaah Akinyi Buyu is a Kenyan politician who has held two different elected positions in the National Assembly on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. She first served as Woman Representative for Kisumu County during the 12th Parliament before moving to contest the Kisumu West constituency seat, which she won in the 2022 general election with 39,658 votes, defeating the incumbent, Olago Aluoch. In the 13th Parliament she sits on the Administration and Internal Security, and Blue Economy, Water and Irrigation committees. Her shift from a county-wide Woman Representative seat to a directly contested constituency seat marks a distinctive step in her political career.

Ruth Adhiambo Odinga is a Kenyan politician who currently serves as the Woman Representative for Kisumu County in the National Assembly, elected on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. Before her legislative career she was Kisumu County's first Deputy Governor, serving from 2013 to 2017. She comes from one of Kenya's most prominent political families, as the daughter of Jaramogi Odinga, the country's first vice president, and sister to both the late former Prime Minister Raila Odinga and Oburu Odinga, the sitting Senator for Siaya County. In the 13th Parliament she sits on the Agriculture and Livestock, Budget and Appropriations, Departmental, and Members Service and Facilities committees.

Ruth Wangari Mwaniki is a Kenyan politician who represented Kigumo constituency in Murang'a County in the National Assembly from 2017 to 2022, serving a single term in the 12th Parliament on the Jubilee Party ticket. During her tenure she was a prominent supporter of President Uhuru Kenyatta and became a vocal advocate for the Building Bridges Initiative (BBI), the constitutional reform process pursued during that period. Her political identity was closely tied to her alignment with the Jubilee administration and its reform agenda throughout her time in the National Assembly.

Ruweida Mohamed Obo is a Kenyan politician born in 1978 on Pate Island, Lamu County. She began her legislative career as Woman Representative for Lamu County in the 12th Parliament on the Jubilee Party ticket, before making history as the first woman elected MP for Lamu East constituency in the 13th Parliament, again on a Jubilee Party ticket. In her current term she sits on the Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers, and Tourism and Wildlife committees. Her transition from a county-wide Woman Representative seat to a directly contested constituency seat marks a notable milestone for women's political representation on the Lamu coast.

Sabina Wanjiru Chege is a Kenyan politician and former television actress and radio presenter who became the inaugural Woman Representative for Murang'a County, serving from 2013 to 2022 across the 11th and 12th Parliaments, first on The National Alliance ticket and later on the Jubilee Party ticket. In the 13th Parliament she sits as a Nominated Member of the National Assembly on the Jubilee Party's list, serving on the Agriculture and Livestock, Departmental, Mediation, and Parliamentary Powers and Privileges committees. She was removed from the position of Deputy Minority Whip in the National Assembly but has continued serving as a nominated MP. Her path into politics followed an earlier career in broadcasting and acting.

Salim Idd Mustafa was a Kenyan broadcast journalist and politician who represented Kilifi South constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. Before entering politics he built a career in broadcast journalism, a background that shaped his public profile ahead of his single term as a legislator. He died in 2020 from suspected complications of COVID-19, cutting short his post-parliamentary life. His career reflects the path several Kenyan media figures have taken from broadcasting into elective office.

Salid Idd Mustafa was a Kenyan broadcast journalist and politician who represented Kilifi South Constituency in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament under the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM). He died in 2020 from suspected COVID-19 complications.

Samson Kiprotich Cherargei is a Kenyan politician who serves as the Senator for Nandi County in the 13th Parliament, elected on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. His election to the Senate placed him among the representatives of Kenya's devolved counties in the upper chamber of Parliament, a body responsible for legislation touching on county governments and their affairs. Public information on his tenure beyond this senatorial role remains limited, with his political identity centered on representing Nandi County in the Senate during the current term.

Samuel Arama is a Kenyan politician who has represented Nakuru Town West constituency in the National Assembly since 2013, winning three consecutive terms across the 11th, 12th, and 13th Parliaments. He was first elected on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket before moving to the Jubilee Party, under which he has been returned in both the 2017 and 2022 general elections. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Departmental, Selection, and Transport and Infrastructure committees. His more than a decade of continuous re-election reflects sustained support from Nakuru Town West's electorate.

Samuel Atandi is a Kenyan politician who has represented Alego Usonga constituency in Siaya County in the National Assembly since 2017, on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. He was re-elected in the 2022 general election, extending his tenure into the 13th Parliament, where he sits on the Budget and Appropriations, Liaison, and Mediation committees. His consistent re-election reflects continued backing from Alego Usonga's voters across two consecutive terms in the National Assembly, cementing his place among ODM's steady base of Nyanza-region legislators.

Samuel Gonzi Rai is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kinango constituency in Kwale County in the National Assembly across five non-consecutive terms since 1992. He was first elected that year on a KANU ticket following the death of his predecessor, Ali Bidu, then re-elected in 2002 and 2007 on the Forum for the Restoration of Democracy-People ticket, and again in 2013 under The National Alliance. After losing the seat in 2017, he reclaimed it in the 2022 general election on the Pamoja African Alliance ticket, now serving in the 13th Parliament on the Departmental, Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers, Energy, and Parliamentary Powers and Privileges committees. Throughout his long career his legislative interests have centered on defence, security, and public accounts.

Samuel Kiprono Chepkonga is a Kenyan politician who has represented Ainabkoi constituency in Uasin Gishu County in the National Assembly across two non-consecutive terms. He was first elected in the 11th Parliament on the United Republican Party ticket, where he served on a Departmental committee, before returning to the seat in the 13th Parliament on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. In his current term he sits on the Budget and Appropriations, Delegated Legislation, House Business, Liaison, and Public Investments committees, reflecting a broad role spanning finance, legislation and parliamentary business.

Samuel Moroto Chumel is a Kenyan politician from the Pokot community of West Pokot County who has represented Kapenguria Constituency in the National Assembly under the Kenya African National Union (KANU), first elected in 2002. Before entering politics, he built a career as a teacher and trade unionist.

Samuel Chumel Moroto is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kapenguria constituency in West Pokot County in the National Assembly since 2002, across four parliamentary terms spanning the 9th, 11th, 12th, and 13th Parliaments. He began his political career on the Kenya African National Union (KANU) ticket, later moving through the Jubilee Party and now the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). Before politics he worked as a career teacher and trade unionist, experience that shaped his long-standing engagement with Pokot community affairs. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Budget and Appropriations, Parliamentary Powers and Privileges, and Social Protection committees, continuing more than two decades of service representing Kapenguria.

Shaaban Ali Isaack is a Kenyan politician who represented two different constituencies in the National Assembly over two parliamentary terms. He was first elected to represent Mandera East constituency in the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket, before later winning the Lafey constituency seat in the 11th Parliament on the United Republican Party ticket. He hails from the Murule clan of the Jidle Hawiye clan of the Somali community, a lineage that has shaped his standing within his home region. Public information on his time in office beyond these two terms remains limited.
Shaaban Ali Issack is a Kenyan politician who served in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament under URP. He hails from the Murule Clan of the Jidle Hawiye clan of the Somali community.

Shakeel Shabbir Ahmed is a Kenyan politician who has represented Kisumu East constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election. He was first elected on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket during that period, later shifting to run as an Independent candidate for his subsequent terms in the 12th and 13th Parliaments. In his current term he sits on the Communication, Information and Innovation, Parliamentary Broadcasting and Library, and Parliamentary Powers and Privileges committees. His move away from party affiliation after an initial ODM-backed election marks a notable feature of his long tenure representing Kisumu East.

Shariff Athman Ali is a Kenyan politician who represented Lamu East constituency in the National Assembly across the 11th and 12th Parliaments. He was first elected in 2013 on the United Democratic Forum ticket with the backing of the Amani Coalition, before later moving to the Jubilee Party for his second term. His two terms in office reflect the shifting coalition politics common on the Kenyan coast during that period, as legislators moved between smaller regional parties and larger national coalitions.

Sheikh Umul Ker Kassim is a Kenyan politician who serves as the Woman Representative for Mandera County in the National Assembly, elected on the United Democratic Movement (UDM) ticket in the 2022 general election. She is a sitting member of the 13th Parliament, running from 2022 to 2027. In her current term she sits on the Departmental, Finance and National Planning, House Business, and Public Investments committees, giving her a role spanning public finance and legislative business. Her election marked her entry into national politics representing Mandera County's women in the National Assembly.

Shukran Hussein Gure is a Kenyan politician who served as the Woman Representative for Garissa County in the National Assembly, elected in March 2013 on the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya ticket during the 11th Parliament. She hails from the Auliyahan subdivision of the Somali Ogaden Darod clan, an ethnic identity that has shaped her standing within her home county. Her single term in office made her one of the early cohort of women representatives elected under Kenya's 2010 constitutional framework, representing Garissa's interests in the National Assembly.

Simeon Nyachae was a Kenyan politician who represented Nyaribari Chache constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on the Forum for the Restoration of Democracy-People ticket. He served as a government minister under both the Moi and Kibaki administrations, giving him influence across two different presidencies. Alongside his political career he was a prominent businessman from Kisii County, with investments spanning London, South Africa, Brazil, Zimbabwe, Australia, and the United States. His combination of cabinet experience and international business interests made him one of the more commercially prominent politicians of his generation.

professional.title: Government Minister
professional.institution: Government of Kenya (Moi and Kibaki administrations)

bio: Sophia Abdi Noor is a Kenyan politician who represented Ijara constituency in the National Assembly on the Party for Democratic Reform ticket, serving into the 12th Parliament. She holds the distinction of being the first woman elected to Kenya's Parliament from the North Eastern region, having first won a seat in the 10th Parliament before her later tenure. During her time in office she served on a Departmental committee. Her breakthrough election marked a significant milestone for women's political representation in a region that had historically elected few female legislators.

Stephen Mogaka is a Kenyan politician and lawyer who has represented West Mugirango constituency in Nyamira County in the National Assembly since his election on 9 August 2022, on the Jubilee Party ticket. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Budget and Appropriations, Decentralized Funds Accounts, Departmental, and Justice and Legal Affairs committees, drawing on his legal background in his legislative work. His election marked his entry into national politics, and he continues to represent West Mugirango's constituents through the current term of the National Assembly.

Stewart Madzayo is a Kenyan politician who serves as the Senator for Kilifi County in the 13th Parliament, elected on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. His election to the Senate placed him among the representatives of Kenya's devolved counties in the upper chamber of Parliament, where senators oversee legislation affecting county governments. Public information on his tenure beyond this senatorial role remains limited, with his political identity centered on representing Kilifi County in the Senate during the current term.

Suleiman Dori Ramadhani was a Kenyan politician who served as Member of Parliament for Msambweni constituency in Kwale County, representing the Orange Democratic Movement across the 11th and 12th Parliaments. His two terms in office placed him among the coastal region's ODM-aligned legislators during that period. Public information on his political career beyond his tenure representing Msambweni remains limited, with his record centered on his service as the constituency's elected representative in the National Assembly across those consecutive parliamentary terms.

Suzanne Ndunge Kiamba is a Kenyan politician who made history as the first woman elected Member of Parliament for Makueni Constituency, winning the seat in August 2022 with 26,909 votes on the Wiper Democratic Movement-Kenya (WDM-K) ticket. She is serving her first term in the 13th Parliament, where she sits on the Departmental, Parliamentary Broadcasting and Library, and Public Petitions committees. Her election marked a significant milestone for women's political representation in Makueni County, an area that had not previously sent a woman to represent the constituency directly in the National Assembly.

Swarup Ranjan Mishra is an Indian-born Kenyan doctor, entrepreneur, and politician who represented Kesses constituency in Uasin Gishu County in the National Assembly from 2017 to 2022, serving a single term in the 12th Parliament on the Jubilee Party ticket. Alongside his political career he is a practicing gynaecologist and the founder and chairman of the Mediheal Group of Hospitals, one of Kenya's prominent private healthcare chains. During his tenure he served on a Departmental committee. His path combined a career in medicine and healthcare entrepreneurship with a single term of elected office representing Kesses's constituents.

Sylvanus Maritim is a Kenyan politician who served as Member of the National Assembly for Ainamoi constituency in Kericho County during the 12th Parliament, elected on the Jubilee Party ticket. He was a member of the ruling Jubilee Party ahead of the 2022 general election before later aligning himself with the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). His single term in office reflects the broader realignment of many Rift Valley politicians from Jubilee toward UDA around that election cycle, a shift that reshaped party loyalties across the region.

Sylvester Wakoli Bifwoli is a Kenyan politician who represented Bumula constituency in Bungoma County in the National Assembly, elected in the 2007 general election as a member of the Ford-Kenya party, an affiliate of the Party of National Unity coalition. During his tenure he served as Assistant Minister in the Ministry of Lands within Kenya's Grand Coalition Government, formed following the disputed 2007 election. His combination of a constituency seat and an assistant ministerial post placed him within both the executive and the legislature during that period of Kenyan politics.

Tabitha Karanja, popularly known by her business name Tabitha Keroche, is a Kenyan businesswoman, entrepreneur, and industrialist who serves as Senator for Nakuru County in the 13th Parliament, elected on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket. Before entering elective politics she founded and led Keroche Breweries as its chief executive officer, building it into the first large brewery in Kenya owned by a non-multinational company, at one point accounting for roughly a fifth of the country's beer consumption. Her transition from a pioneering industrialist to a sitting senator marks a distinctive path into national politics for a prominent Kenyan businesswoman.

Timothy Wanyonyi Wetangula, commonly known as Tim Wanyonyi, is a Kenyan lawyer and politician who has represented Westlands constituency in Nairobi County in the National Assembly since 2013, across three consecutive terms on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket. Before winning the Westlands seat he served as a councillor in the now-defunct Nairobi City Council following the 2007 election. He defended his parliamentary seat in 2017 and again in 2022, when he defeated Nelson Havi. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Departmental, Justice and Legal Affairs, and Social Protection committees, having previously chaired a Departmental committee in the 12th Parliament. He is the brother of Moses Wetangula, the Speaker of the National Assembly and a former Senator for Bungoma County.

Tecla Chebet Tum is a Kenyan politician who served as the Woman Representative for Nandi County in the National Assembly during the 12th Parliament, elected on the Jubilee Party ticket before later aligning with the United Democratic Alliance (UDA). After leaving elected office she moved into public service in the executive, taking up the role of Director of Gender in the Office of the President. Her career thus spans a single term of directly elected representation followed by an appointed administrative role focused on gender affairs at the national level.

Uhuru Muigai Kenyatta is a Kenyan politician who represented Gatundu South constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket, and is the son of Jomo Kenyatta, Kenya's first president. He served as leader of the official opposition from 2003 to 2007 before becoming Deputy Prime Minister from 2008 to 2013. He went on to serve as Kenya's fourth president from 2013 to 2022, capping a political career that moved from a single constituency seat through opposition leadership and the deputy premiership to the country's highest office.

Victor Kioko Munyaka is a Kenyan politician who has represented Machakos Town constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election, across terms including the 11th Parliament on the Chama Cha Uzalendo ticket and the 12th Parliament on the Jubilee Party ticket. In the 12th Parliament he served on a Departmental committee. He holds a degree in Veterinary Medicine from the University of Nairobi, a professional qualification that preceded his long-running political career representing Machakos Town's constituents across multiple parliamentary terms.

Dr Victor Kioko Munyaka is a Kenyan politician who has represented Machakos Town Constituency in the National Assembly since the 2007 general election, serving in the 11th Parliament under CCU and the 12th Parliament under the Jubilee Party. He holds a degree in Veterinary Medicine from the University of Nairobi and has served on several departmental committees during his tenure.

Athanas Misiko Wafula Wamunyinyi is a Kenyan politician who first entered Parliament in 1997, winning the Kanduyi constituency seat in Bungoma County while still in his twenties, and was re-elected in 2002. He lost the seat in the contested 2007 general election but returned to Parliament in 2013, going on to serve two further consecutive terms through the 11th and 12th Parliaments under the Forum for the Restoration of Democracy-Kenya ticket, having earlier run on the National Rainbow Coalition banner. During this later period he served on the Public Investments committee, and later the Departmental and Select committees. He also leads the Democratic Action Party, extending his political influence beyond his constituency seat.

Wangari Muta Maathai was a Kenyan social, environmental, and political activist who represented Tetu constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on the National Rainbow Coalition ticket. Long before her political career she founded the Green Belt Movement, an environmental organization dedicated to tree planting, conservation, and women's rights, work that brought her international recognition. In 2004 she became the first African woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize, a milestone that cemented her legacy well beyond her single term as an elected legislator. Her combined record as an environmental pioneer and Nobel laureate remains one of the most celebrated in Kenya's modern history.

Wanjiku Muhia is a Kenyan politician who made history as the first female representative for Nyandarua County, serving as Woman Representative from 2013 to 2017 in the 11th Parliament on The National Alliance ticket. She went on to serve as a regional Member of Parliament in the 4th East African Legislative Assembly from 2018 to 2022, before returning to national politics by winning the Kipipiri constituency seat in the 2022 general election on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket, defeating incumbent Amos Kimunya. In the 13th Parliament she chairs the Regional Integration Committee and also sits on the Joint Select and Select committees, drawing on her earlier East African Community experience.

Washington Jakoyo Midiwo was a Kenyan politician who represented Gem constituency in Siaya County in the National Assembly from 2002 until 2017, spanning multiple parliamentary terms including the 9th and 11th Parliaments. He began his tenure aligned with the National Rainbow Coalition before becoming a long-serving member of the Orange Democratic Movement. During the 11th Parliament he served on a Departmental committee. His fifteen years representing Gem made him one of Nyanza's more enduring legislators of that era, spanning several general elections and shifting coalition politics.

Wesley Kipchumba Korir is a Kenyan long-distance runner and politician who represented Cherangany constituency in the National Assembly as an independent candidate following his election in the 2013 general election, before losing the seat in 2017. Before entering politics he built an acclaimed athletic career, winning the Boston Marathon in April 2012 with a time of 2 hours 12 minutes 40 seconds, and earlier taking back-to-back victories at the Los Angeles Marathon in 2009 and 2010, the first runner to do so there in eight years. He also finished second at the 2011 Chicago Marathon in a personal-best 2 hours 6 minutes 15 seconds, improving that mark slightly the following year. His single term in Parliament followed one of the most decorated marathon careers among Kenyan athletes of his generation.

Wilberforce Ojiambo Oundo is a Kenyan politician who has represented Funyula constituency in Busia County in the National Assembly since 2017, on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket, and was re-elected in 2022 for the 13th Parliament. Before entering politics he worked as a Real Estate lecturer at the University of Nairobi. In the 13th Parliament he chairs the Public Accounts committee, having earlier served on a Departmental committee during the 12th Parliament. His move from academia into two consecutive terms representing Funyula reflects a shift from a teaching career into national legislative work.

Wilfred Gisuka Machage was a Kenyan politician who represented Kuria constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on the National Rainbow Coalition ticket. Public information on his political career beyond this single term in office is limited. His tenure placed him among the cohort of legislators elected under the NARC banner following the 2002 general election, representing Kuria's constituents in that Parliament. Little else is recorded of his activities in office or his subsequent public life beyond this period of service.

William Kipkorir was a Kenyan politician who represented Baringo North constituency in the National Assembly for three terms, spanning the 10th, 11th, and 12th Parliaments, moving through parties including the United Republican Party and later the Jubilee Party. During the 12th Parliament he served on a Departmental committee. In the 2022 general election he moved to the Senate, winning the Baringo County seat on the United Democratic Alliance (UDA) ticket after defeating Gideon Moi. He died on 15 February 2025 at the age of 57, while serving as senator, closing out a political career spanning both chambers of Kenya's Parliament.

William Kabogo Gitau is a Kenyan politician who represented Juja constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on the Sisi Kwa Sisi ticket. He went on to become the first Governor of Kiambu County, a position he won in the 2013 general election, before later moving into national government as Cabinet Secretary for Information, Communications and the Digital Economy. He also leads the Tujibebe Wakenya Party. His career thus progressed from a single term as a backbench MP to leading a devolved county government and then into a cabinet-level executive role.

professional.title: Cabinet Secretary for Information, Communications and the Digital Economy

bio: William Kamoti Mwamkale was a Kenyan lawyer and politician who became the first Member of Parliament for Rabai constituency in Kilifi County following its creation under Kenya's 2010 Constitution, elected in 2013 on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket and serving across the 11th and 12th Parliaments. In the National Assembly he sat on the Departmental Committee on Justice and Legal Affairs. He also sponsored the 2014 Rabai Super Cup football tournament as an initiative to promote peace among his constituents, reflecting his interest in community cohesion alongside his legislative duties.

William Kamuren Chirchir Chepkut was a Kenyan politician who served as Member of the National Assembly for Ainabkoi constituency in Uasin Gishu County between 2017 and 2022, running as an independent candidate during the 12th Parliament. He lost his bid for re-election in the 2022 general election, being defeated by Samuel Chepkonga, who went on to hold the seat in the following parliament. His single term in office reflects the challenges independent candidates often face in retaining seats against party-backed opponents in subsequent elections.

William Samoei Ruto is a Kenyan politician who represented Eldoret North constituency in the National Assembly during the 9th Parliament on a KANU ticket. He went on to hold three cabinet portfolios, serving as Minister for Home Affairs, Minister of Agriculture, and Minister for Higher Education, before becoming Kenya's first elected Deputy President, a role he held from 2013 to 2022. He has served as Kenya's fifth and current president since 13 September 2022. His career thus progressed from a single constituency seat through multiple cabinet positions and the deputy presidency to the country's highest office.

Yusuf Hassan Abdi is a Kenyan politician, diplomat, social activist, and former journalist who has represented Kamukunji constituency in Nairobi County in the National Assembly since first entering Parliament through a by-election in 2011. He was elected in his own right in the 11th Parliament on The National Alliance ticket, then re-elected in the 12th and 13th Parliaments on the Jubilee Party ticket, most recently in the 2022 general election. Before politics he worked for many years with the United Nations. In the 13th Parliament he sits on the Defence, Intelligence and Foreign Relations, and National Cohesion and Equal Opportunity committees, roles that draw on his earlier diplomatic and humanitarian background.

Yusuf Kifuma Chanzu is a Kenyan politician who represented Vihiga constituency in the National Assembly across two non-consecutive periods, from 1997 to 2002 and again from 2007 to 2013, with his later term falling within the 11th Parliament on the United Democratic Forum ticket. His two separate stretches in office, separated by a term out of Parliament, reflect the shifting electoral fortunes common among western Kenya legislators of that era. Public information on his political activities between and beyond these terms remains limited.

Zakayo Cheruiyot is a Kenyan politician who represented Kuresoi South constituency in Nakuru County in the National Assembly during the 11th Parliament, elected on the United Republican Party ticket. Public information on his background and activities beyond this single term in office is limited. His tenure placed him among the cohort of legislators elected in the 2013 general election under the URP banner, representing Kuresoi South's constituents in that Parliament, a period marked by the party's strong showing across much of the Rift Valley region.

Zamzam Mohamed is a Kenyan politician who serves as the Woman Representative for Mombasa County in the National Assembly, elected on the Orange Democratic Movement (ODM) ticket for the 13th Parliament. In her current term she sits on the Implementation, Departmental, Diaspora Affairs and Migrant Workers, and Social Protection committees, giving her a role spanning oversight, migration issues, and social policy. Public information on her background beyond this legislative role remains limited, with her political record centered on her representation of Mombasa's women in the current Parliament.

Zuleikha Juma Hassan is a Kenyan politician who has served in Kenya's National Assembly across two different types of parliamentary seats, both on the Orange Democratic Movement ticket. She was first a Nominated Member of Parliament in the 11th Parliament before being directly elected as Woman Representative for Kwale County in the 12th Parliament, where she served on a Departmental committee. Her progression from a nominated seat to a directly elected county-wide position reflects a common pathway for women entering Kenyan elective politics under the 2010 constitutional framework.
